API Reference¶

This section details the Python API of PyGomo.

Top Level¶

PyGomo - Python Gomoku Engine Communication Framework

A clean, extensible library for communicating with Gomoku engines via the Gomocup protocol.

Example

from pygomo import EngineClient

with EngineClient(“/path/to/engine”) as engine:

engine.start(15)

# With realtime search info def on_info(info):

print(f”Depth {info.depth}: {info.winrate_percent:.1f}%”)

result = engine.turn(“h8”, on_info=on_info) print(f”Engine played: {result.move}”)

engine.quit()

class pygomo.BitBoard(_size: int = 15, _black: int = 0, _white: int = 0, _move_count: int = 0, _history: list[Move] = <factory>, _hash: int = 0, _zobrist: ZobristHash = None)[source]¶

Bases: IBoard

BitBoard representation for Gomoku.

Uses two Python integers as bitboards: - _black: Bit set for each black stone - _white: Bit set for each white stone

For a 15x15 board, each bitboard is 225 bits. Bit index = row * size + col.

Features: - O(1) place, remove, is_empty operations - O(1) win detection using bit shifting - Zobrist hashing for transposition tables - Move history tracking

Example

board = BitBoard(size=15) board.place(Move(“h8”)) # Black plays board.place(Move(“i8”)) # White plays board.place(Move(“h7”)) # Black plays

if board.check_win(Move(“h8”)):

print(“Black wins!”)

check_win(move: Move | None = None) → WinInfo | None[source]¶

Check for a winning condition.

Uses bit shifting to detect 5-in-a-row patterns. If move is provided, only checks lines through that position.

Create an independent copy of the board.

class pygomo.CommandRegistry[source]¶

Bases: object

Central registry for command handlers.

Provides:

  • Handler registration and lookup

  • Alias support

  • Hook integration

  • Plugin-style command extension

Example

registry = CommandRegistry() registry.register(TurnHandler()) registry.register(StartHandler())

# Execute a command result = registry.execute(context)

clear() → None[source]¶

Remove all registered handlers.

execute(context: CommandContext) → CommandResult[source]¶

Execute a command using its registered handler.

Parameters:

context – Command execution context.

Returns:

CommandResult from handler.

get(command: str) → ICommandHandler | None[source]¶

Get handler for a command.

Parameters:

command – Command name or alias.

Returns:

Handler if found, None otherwise.

has(command: str) → bool[source]¶

Check if a command has a registered handler.

property hooks: HookManager¶

Get the hook manager.

list_all() → list[str][source]¶

Get list of all commands including aliases.

list_commands() → list[str][source]¶

Get list of all registered command names.

register(handler: ICommandHandler) → None[source]¶

Register a command handler.

Parameters:

handler – Handler instance to register.

Raises:

ValueError – If handler for command already exists.

register_class(handler_class: Type[ICommandHandler]) → None[source]¶

Register a handler by class (instantiates automatically).

Parameters:

handler_class – Handler class to instantiate and register.

unregister(command: str) → bool[source]¶

Unregister a command handler.

Parameters:

command – Command name to unregister.

Returns:

True if handler was removed, False if not found.

class pygomo.EngineClient(executable_path: str, protocol: IProtocol | None = None, auto_start: bool = False, **transport_kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: object

High-level client for engine communication.

Provides a clean, user-friendly API for interacting with Gomoku engines via the Gomocup protocol.

Example:

# Basic usage
with EngineClient("/path/to/engine") as engine:
    engine.start(15)

    # Make a move and get response
    result = engine.turn("h8")
    print(f"Engine played: {result.move}")

    # With realtime callbacks
    def on_info(info: SearchInfo):
        print(f"Depth {info.depth}: {info.winrate_percent:.1f}%")

    result = engine.turn("i9", on_info=on_info)

    engine.quit()

Advanced usage:

# Access hooks for custom processing
engine.hooks.on(HookType.PRE_EXECUTE)(my_hook)

# Register custom command handler
engine.registry.register(MyCustomHandler())

# Raw command execution
engine.execute("MYCOMMAND", arg1, arg2)
about(timeout: float | None = None) → str | None[source]¶

Get engine information.

Returns:

Engine info string, or None on failure.

begin(timeout: float | None = None, on_info: Callable[[SearchInfo], None] | None = None) → PlayResult | None[source]¶

Request engine’s first move (engine plays black).

Parameters:

  • timeout – Maximum thinking time.

  • on_info – Callback for realtime search info.

Returns:

PlayResult with engine’s move, or None on failure.

board(position: BoardPosition, start_thinking: bool = True, timeout: float | None = None, on_info: Callable[[SearchInfo], None] | None = None) → PlayResult | None[source]¶

Set up a position and optionally get engine’s move.

Parameters:

  • position – BoardPosition with moves.

  • start_thinking – Whether to trigger engine thinking.

  • timeout – Maximum thinking time.

  • on_info – Callback for realtime search info.

Returns:

PlayResult if thinking, None otherwise.

configure(**options) → None[source]¶

Configure engine options.

Parameters:

**options – Configuration options as keyword arguments.

Example:

engine.configure(
    timeout_turn=5000,
    timeout_match=300000,
    max_memory=1073741824,
    thread_num=4,
)

Connect to the engine (start subprocess).

Raises:

timeout – Time to wait for graceful shutdown.

Parameters:

class pygomo.Evaluate(raw_value: str, DEFAULT_SCALING_FACTOR: float = 400.0)[source]¶

Bases: object

Engine evaluation score with winrate conversion.

Supports both centipawn scores and mate scores.

DEFAULT_SCALING_FACTOR: float = 400.0¶
is_losing() → bool[source]¶

Check if position is losing (negative mate).

is_mate() → bool[source]¶

Check if this is a mate score.

is_winning() → bool[source]¶

Check if position is winning (positive mate).

raw_value: str¶
score() → int | float | MateScore[source]¶

Get the evaluation score.

Returns:

Numeric score, or MateScore for mate positions.

winrate(scaling_factor: float | None = None) → float[source]¶

Convert evaluation to winrate using sigmoid function.

Formula: winrate = 1 / (1 + e^(-eval / scaling_factor))

Parameters:

scaling_factor – Controls curve steepness. Higher = flatter.

Returns:

Winrate in range [0, 1].

winrate_percent(scaling_factor: float | None = None) → float[source]¶

Get winrate as percentage [0, 100].

class pygomo.GomocupProtocol[source]¶

Bases: IProtocol

Gomocup (Piskvork) protocol implementation.

Handles serialization and parsing of Gomocup protocol messages. Supports both standard Gomocup and Yixin-Board extensions.

Example

protocol = GomocupProtocol() cmd = protocol.serialize_command(“TURN”, “7,8”) # Output: “TURN 7,8”

RESPONSE_TYPES = {'ABOUT': ResponseType.TEXT, 'BEGIN': ResponseType.COORD, 'BOARD': ResponseType.COORD, 'END': ResponseType.NONE, 'INFO': ResponseType.NONE, 'RESTART': ResponseType.OK, 'START': ResponseType.OK, 'STOP': ResponseType.NONE, 'TAKEBACK': ResponseType.OK, 'TURN': ResponseType.COORD, 'YXBOARD': ResponseType.NONE, 'YXNBEST': ResponseType.COORD, 'YXSHOWFORBID': ResponseType.TEXT, 'YXSHOWINFO': ResponseType.MESSAGE, 'YXSTOP': ResponseType.NONE}¶
THINKING_COMMANDS = frozenset({'BEGIN', 'BOARD', 'SWAP2BOARD', 'TURN', 'YXBALANCEONE', 'YXBALANCETWO', 'YXNBEST', 'YXSEARCHDEFEND'})¶
format_board_position(moves: list[tuple[Move, int]], start_thinking: bool = True) → list[str][source]¶

Parameters:

Check if command triggers engine thinking.

Protocol name identifier.

parse_coord(data: str) → tuple[int, int][source]¶

Parse coordinate string to (x, y).

Parameters:

data – Coordinate string like “7,8”.

Returns:

Tuple of (x, y).

parse_message(data: str) → dict[str, Any][source]¶

Parse a MESSAGE line into structured data.

Handles multiple formats from Rapfi: - UCILIKE: depth X-Y ev VAL n NODES n/ms SPEED tm TIME pv MOVES - UCILIKE MCTS: multipv X ev VAL w WR d DR stdev SD v V seldepth SD n N n/ms SPEED tm TIME prior P pv MOVES - NORMAL: Depth X-Y | Eval VAL | Time T | MOVES

Parameters:

data – Raw MESSAGE line.

Returns:

Dictionary with parsed fields.

parse_search_info(data: str) → SearchInfo[source]¶

Parse MESSAGE line into SearchInfo object.

Parameters:

data – Raw MESSAGE line.

Returns:

SearchInfo with parsed data.

serialize_command(command: str, *args: Any) → str[source]¶

Serialize a command with arguments.

Parameters:

  • command – Command name.

  • *args – Command arguments.

Returns:

Formatted command string.

class pygomo.HookManager[source]¶

Bases: object

Manages hooks for command lifecycle events.

Supports both class-based hooks (IHook) and simple functions.

Example

manager = HookManager()

# Register a function hook @manager.on(HookType.PRE_EXECUTE) def log_command(context, _):

print(f”Executing: {context.command}”)

# Register a class hook manager.add(MyHook())

add(hook: IHook) → None[source]¶

Add a hook.

Parameters:

hook – Hook instance to add.

add_function(hook_type: HookType, func: Callable[[CommandContext, Any | None], None], priority: int = 100) → None[source]¶

Add a function as a hook.

Parameters:

  • hook_type – When to execute.

  • func – Function to call.

  • priority – Execution order (lower = first).

clear(hook_type: HookType | None = None) → None[source]¶

Clear hooks.

Parameters:

hook_type – Specific type to clear, or None for all.

count(hook_type: HookType | None = None) → int[source]¶

Get number of registered hooks.

on(hook_type: HookType, priority: int = 100) → Callable[[Callable[[CommandContext, Any | None], None]], Callable[[CommandContext, Any | None], None]][source]¶

Decorator for registering function hooks.

Example

@manager.on(HookType.PRE_EXECUTE) def my_hook(context, result):

pass

remove(hook: IHook | Callable[[CommandContext, Any | None], None]) → bool[source]¶

Remove a hook.

Parameters:

hook – Hook to remove.

Returns:

True if removed, False if not found.

run(hook_type: HookType, context: CommandContext, result_or_error: Any = None) → None[source]¶

Run all hooks of a specific type.

Parameters:

  • hook_type – Type of hooks to run.

  • context – Command context.

  • result_or_error – Result or error for post/error hooks.

class pygomo.IRenjuBoard[source]¶

Bases: IBoard

Extended interface for Renju rule support.

Adds forbidden move detection for Black.

abstractmethod get_forbidden_moves() → list[Move][source]¶

Get all currently forbidden moves for Black.

abstractmethod is_forbidden(move: Move) → bool[source]¶

Check if a move is forbidden for Black (Renju rules).

Forbidden patterns: - Double-three (two open threes) - Double-four (two fours) - Overline (6+ in a row)

Note: Only applies to Black. White has no restrictions.

Returns:

True if forbidden, False otherwise.

class pygomo.Move(move: tuple[int, int] | str)[source]¶

Bases: object

Represents a board position/move.

Supports multiple input formats:

  • Tuple: (7, 8)

  • String numeric: “7,8”

  • String algebraic: “h8”

Coordinates are 0-indexed internally.

col: int¶
row: int¶
to_algebraic() → str[source]¶

Return as algebraic notation ‘a1’ style.

to_numeric() → str[source]¶

Return as numeric string ‘col,row’.

to_tuple() → tuple[int, int][source]¶

Return as (col, row) tuple.

class pygomo.OutputChannelRouter(stream: TextIO)[source]¶

Bases: object

Routes engine stdout lines to categorized channels.

Each channel has a filter function that determines which lines belong to it. Lines are read in a background thread and distributed to the appropriate queues.

Default channels:

  • coord: Lines matching coordinate pattern (e.g., “7,8”)

  • message: Lines starting with “MESSAGE”

  • info: Lines starting with “INFO”

  • error: Lines starting with “ERROR”

  • output: All other non-error lines

Example

router = OutputChannelRouter(process.stdout)

# Get a coordinate response coord = router.get(“coord”, timeout=5.0)

# Get realtime search info message = router.get(“message”, timeout=0.1)

COORD_PATTERN = re.compile('^\\d+\\s*,\\s*\\d+(\\s+\\d+\\s*,\\s*\\d+)*$')¶
add_channel(name: str, filter_func: Callable[[str], bool]) → None[source]¶

Add a new output channel.

Parameters:

  • name – Channel name for retrieval.

  • filter_func – Function that returns True if a line belongs to this channel.

Raises:

ValueError – If channel already exists.

property channels: list[str]¶

Get list of available channel names.

clear(channel: str) → None[source]¶

Clear all pending messages in a channel.

Parameters:

channel – Channel name to clear.

clear_all() → None[source]¶

Clear all pending messages in all channels.

get(channel: str, timeout: float = 0.0, reset: bool = False) → str[source]¶

Get the next line from a channel.

Parameters:

  • channel – Channel name to read from.

  • timeout – Maximum time to wait in seconds. 0 means no wait.

  • reset – If True, clear old messages before reading.

Returns:

The next line, or empty string on timeout.

Raises:

ValueError – If channel doesn’t exist.

get_all(channel: str) → list[str][source]¶

Get all available lines from a channel.

Parameters:

channel – Channel name to read from.

Returns:

List of all available lines.

get_nowait(channel: str) → str | None[source]¶

Get a line without waiting.

Parameters:

channel – Channel name to read from.

Returns:

The next line, or None if queue is empty.

remove_channel(name: str) → None[source]¶

Remove an output channel.

Parameters:

name – Channel name to remove.

stop() → None[source]¶

Stop the background reader thread.

move: Move¶

search_info: SearchInfo | None = None¶

Get winrate from search info.

exception pygomo.ProtocolError[source]¶

Bases: PyGomoError

Error related to protocol parsing or serialization.

exception pygomo.PyGomoError[source]¶

Bases: Exception

Base exception for all PyGomo errors.

pygomo.PyGomoTimeoutError¶

alias of TimeoutError

class pygomo.RenjuBitBoard(_size: int = 15, _black: int = 0, _white: int = 0, _move_count: int = 0, _history: list[Move] = <factory>, _hash: int = 0, _zobrist: ZobristHash = None)[source]¶

Bases: BitBoard, IRenjuBoard

BitBoard with Renju forbidden move detection.

In Renju, Black (the first player) has restrictions: - Overline (6+): Forbidden - Double-four: Forbidden (two fours in different directions) - Double-three: Forbidden (two open threes in different directions)

White has no restrictions and wins with 5 or more.

Example:

board = RenjuBitBoard(size=15)
board.place(Move("h8"))  # Black

# Check if a move would be forbidden
if board.is_forbidden(Move("i9")):
    print("Move is forbidden!")
check_win(move: Move | None = None) → WinInfo | None[source]¶

Check for win with Renju overline rule.

Black’s 6+ in a row is forbidden (not a win). White’s 5+ is a win.

copy() → RenjuBitBoard[source]¶

Create an independent copy.

get_forbidden_moves() → list[Move][source]¶

Get all currently forbidden moves for Black.

is_forbidden(move: Move) → bool[source]¶

Check if a move is forbidden for Black (Renju rules).

Only applies when it’s Black’s turn.

Parameters:

move – The move to check.

Returns:

True if the move is forbidden for Black.

place(move: Move, color: int | None = None) → bool[source]¶

Place a stone, respecting Renju forbidden rules.

If it’s Black’s turn and the move is forbidden, return False.

class pygomo.SubprocessTransport(executable_path: str, args: list[str] | None = None, working_directory: str | None = None)[source]¶

Bases: ITransport

Transport implementation using Python subprocess.

Spawns an engine executable and provides bidirectional communication via stdin/stdout pipes.

Example

transport = SubprocessTransport(“/path/to/engine”) transport.start() transport.send(“START 15”) response = transport.receive(timeout=5.0) transport.stop()

get_reader() → IStreamReader[source]¶

Get a reader for the default channel.

get_router() → OutputChannelRouter[source]¶

Get the output channel router for advanced usage.

Returns:

The OutputChannelRouter instance.

get_writer() → IStreamWriter[source]¶

Get the stdin writer.

property is_running: bool¶

Check if the engine process is running.

property process_id: int | None¶

Get the process ID of the engine.

receive(timeout: float | None = None) → str[source]¶

Receive a line from the engine’s default output.

Parameters:

timeout – Timeout in seconds.

Returns:

The received line.

send(data: str) → None[source]¶

Send a command to the engine.

Parameters:

data – Command string (newline appended automatically).

start() → None[source]¶

Start the engine subprocess.

Raises:

  • TransportError – If already running.

  • TransportConnectionError – If process fails to start.

stop(timeout: float = 5.0) → None[source]¶

Stop the engine subprocess.

Attempts graceful shutdown first, then forces termination.

Parameters:

timeout – Time to wait for graceful shutdown.

Base exception for transport layer errors.

exception pygomo.TransportTimeoutError[source]¶

Bases: TransportError

Raised when a transport operation times out.

Client¶

Application layer provides high-level API for engine interaction.

class pygomo.client.EngineClient(executable_path: str, protocol: IProtocol | None = None, auto_start: bool = False, **transport_kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: object

High-level client for engine communication.

Provides a clean, user-friendly API for interacting with Gomoku engines via the Gomocup protocol.

Example:

# Basic usage
with EngineClient("/path/to/engine") as engine:
    engine.start(15)

    # Make a move and get response
    result = engine.turn("h8")
    print(f"Engine played: {result.move}")

    # With realtime callbacks
    def on_info(info: SearchInfo):
        print(f"Depth {info.depth}: {info.winrate_percent:.1f}%")

    result = engine.turn("i9", on_info=on_info)

    engine.quit()

Advanced usage:

# Access hooks for custom processing
engine.hooks.on(HookType.PRE_EXECUTE)(my_hook)

# Register custom command handler
engine.registry.register(MyCustomHandler())

# Raw command execution
engine.execute("MYCOMMAND", arg1, arg2)
about(timeout: float | None = None) → str | None[source]¶

Get engine information.

Returns:

Engine info string, or None on failure.

begin(timeout: float | None = None, on_info: Callable[[SearchInfo], None] | None = None) → PlayResult | None[source]¶

Request engine’s first move (engine plays black).

Parameters:

  • timeout – Maximum thinking time.

  • on_info – Callback for realtime search info.

Returns:

PlayResult with engine’s move, or None on failure.

board(position: BoardPosition, start_thinking: bool = True, timeout: float | None = None, on_info: Callable[[SearchInfo], None] | None = None) → PlayResult | None[source]¶

Set up a position and optionally get engine’s move.

Parameters:

  • position – BoardPosition with moves.

  • start_thinking – Whether to trigger engine thinking.

  • timeout – Maximum thinking time.

  • on_info – Callback for realtime search info.

Returns:

PlayResult if thinking, None otherwise.

configure(**options) → None[source]¶

Configure engine options.

Parameters:

**options – Configuration options as keyword arguments.

Example:

engine.configure(
    timeout_turn=5000,
    timeout_match=300000,
    max_memory=1073741824,
    thread_num=4,
)
connect() → None[source]¶

Connect to the engine (start subprocess).

Raises:

RuntimeError – If already connected.

disconnect(timeout: float = 5.0) → None[source]¶

Disconnect from engine (stop subprocess).

Parameters:

timeout – Time to wait for graceful shutdown.

execute(command: str, *args, timeout: float | None = None, on_info: Callable[[SearchInfo], None] | None = None, **kwargs) → CommandResult[source]¶

Execute a raw command.

Parameters:

  • command – Command name.

  • *args – Positional arguments.

  • timeout – Command timeout.

  • on_info – Callback for search info.

  • **kwargs – Keyword arguments.

Returns:

CommandResult from handler.

Check if connected to engine.

property is_started: bool¶

Check if game has been started (START command sent).

Get multiple best moves from engine.

Parameters:

  • timeout – Maximum thinking time.

Returns:

PlayResult with best move and all info.

Quit the engine and disconnect.

True if successful.

turn(move: str | Move | tuple[int, int], timeout: float | None = None, on_info: Callable[[SearchInfo], None] | None = None) → PlayResult | None[source]¶

Send opponent’s move and get engine’s response.

Parameters:

  • move – Move in any format (“h8”, “7,8”, (7, 8), or Move).

  • timeout – Maximum thinking time.

  • on_info – Callback for realtime search info.

Returns:

PlayResult with engine’s move, or None on failure.

Board¶

PyGomo Board Submodule.

Provides board representations for Gomoku: - BitBoard: Fast bitwise operations - RenjuBitBoard: With forbidden move detection

Example

from pygomo.board import BitBoard, RenjuBitBoard

# Standard board board = BitBoard(size=15) board.place(Move(“h8”))

# Renju with forbidden detection renju = RenjuBitBoard(size=15) if renju.is_forbidden(Move(“i9”)):

print(“Forbidden!”)

class pygomo.board.BitBoard(_size: int = 15, _black: int = 0, _white: int = 0, _move_count: int = 0, _history: list[Move] = <factory>, _hash: int = 0, _zobrist: ZobristHash = None)[source]¶

Bases: IBoard

BitBoard representation for Gomoku.

Uses two Python integers as bitboards: - _black: Bit set for each black stone - _white: Bit set for each white stone

For a 15x15 board, each bitboard is 225 bits. Bit index = row * size + col.

Features: - O(1) place, remove, is_empty operations - O(1) win detection using bit shifting - Zobrist hashing for transposition tables - Move history tracking

Example

board = BitBoard(size=15) board.place(Move(“h8”)) # Black plays board.place(Move(“i8”)) # White plays board.place(Move(“h7”)) # Black plays

if board.check_win(Move(“h8”)):

print(“Black wins!”)

check_win(move: Move | None = None) → WinInfo | None[source]¶

Check for a winning condition.

Uses bit shifting to detect 5-in-a-row patterns. If move is provided, only checks lines through that position.

copy() → BitBoard[source]¶

Create an independent copy of the board.

property current_player: int¶

Current player to move (BLACK or WHITE).

get(move: Move) → int[source]¶

Get the stone at position.

get_legal_moves() → list[Move][source]¶

Get all legal (empty) positions.

get_move_history() → list[Move][source]¶

Get ordered list of moves played.

property hash: int¶

Zobrist hash of current position.

is_empty(move: Move) → bool[source]¶

Check if position is empty.

is_full() → bool[source]¶

Check if board is completely filled.

is_valid(move: Move) → bool[source]¶

Check if position is within board bounds.

property last_move: Move | None¶

Last move played, or None if empty.

property move_count: int¶

Number of moves played.

place(move: Move, color: int | None = None) → bool[source]¶

Place a stone on the board.

remove(move: Move) → bool[source]¶

Remove a stone from the board.

property size: int¶

Board size (e.g., 15 for 15x15).

undo() → Move | None[source]¶

Undo the last move.

class pygomo.board.IBoard[source]¶

Bases: ABC

Abstract interface for Gomoku board implementations.

Provides a common API for different board representations (BitBoard, SimpleBoard, etc.).

BLACK = 1¶
EMPTY = 0¶
WHITE = 2¶

Check for a winning condition.

move – Check around this move (optimization). If None, check entire board.

WinInfo if there’s a winner, None otherwise.

Place a stone on the board.

  • color – Stone color (defaults to current player).

True if successful, False if invalid.

move – Position to remove.

Returns:

True if successful, False if empty.

Board size (e.g., 15 for 15x15).

Iterate over all stones of a given color.

The undone move, or None if no moves to undo.

class pygomo.board.IRenjuBoard[source]¶

Bases: IBoard

Extended interface for Renju rule support.

Adds forbidden move detection for Black.

abstractmethod get_forbidden_moves() → list[Move][source]¶

Get all currently forbidden moves for Black.

abstractmethod is_forbidden(move: Move) → bool[source]¶

Check if a move is forbidden for Black (Renju rules).

Forbidden patterns: - Double-three (two open threes) - Double-four (two fours) - Overline (6+ in a row)

Note: Only applies to Black. White has no restrictions.

Returns:

True if forbidden, False otherwise.

class pygomo.board.RenjuBitBoard(_size: int = 15, _black: int = 0, _white: int = 0, _move_count: int = 0, _history: list[Move] = <factory>, _hash: int = 0, _zobrist: ZobristHash = None)[source]¶

Bases: BitBoard, IRenjuBoard

BitBoard with Renju forbidden move detection.

In Renju, Black (the first player) has restrictions: - Overline (6+): Forbidden - Double-four: Forbidden (two fours in different directions) - Double-three: Forbidden (two open threes in different directions)

White has no restrictions and wins with 5 or more.

Example:

board = RenjuBitBoard(size=15)
board.place(Move("h8"))  # Black

# Check if a move would be forbidden
if board.is_forbidden(Move("i9")):
    print("Move is forbidden!")

Check for win with Renju overline rule.

Black’s 6+ in a row is forbidden (not a win). White’s 5+ is a win.

class pygomo.board.WinInfo(winner: int, line: list[Move], direction: str)[source]¶

Bases: object

Information about a winning line.

direction: str¶
line: list[Move]¶
winner: int¶
class pygomo.board.ZobristHash(size: int = 15, seed: int | None = None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Zobrist hashing for Gomoku boards.

Uses random 64-bit integers for each (position, color) pair. Hash is XORed incrementally as stones are added/removed.

Example

zobrist = ZobristHash(size=15)

hash_val = zobrist.empty_hash hash_val = zobrist.update(hash_val, Move(“h8”), BLACK) # Add stone hash_val = zobrist.update(hash_val, Move(“h8”), BLACK) # Remove stone (XOR again)

compute_full(stones: list[tuple[int, int, int]]) → int[source]¶

Compute hash from scratch for a list of stones.

Parameters:

stones – List of (col, row, color) tuples.

Returns:

Complete hash value.

property empty_hash: int¶

Hash value for an empty board.

get_value(col: int, row: int, color: int) → int[source]¶

Get the Zobrist value for a position and color.

Parameters:

  • col – Column (0-indexed).

  • row – Row (0-indexed).

  • color – Stone color (1=BLACK, 2=WHITE).

Returns:

64-bit random integer for this (position, color).

toggle_side(current_hash: int) → int[source]¶

Toggle the side-to-move bit.

Optional: use if you want different hashes for same position but different side to move.

update(current_hash: int, col: int, row: int, color: int) → int[source]¶

Update hash when adding or removing a stone.

XOR is self-inverse: adding then removing gives original hash.

Parameters:

  • current_hash – Current hash value.

  • col – Column of stone.

  • row – Row of stone.

  • color – Color of stone.

Returns:

Updated hash value.

pygomo.board.get_zobrist(size: int = 15) → ZobristHash[source]¶

Get a Zobrist hash instance for the given board size.

Uses a cache to reuse instances for the same size.

Protocol¶

Protocol layer provides message serialization and parsing for engine communication.

Represents a board position with move history.

Used for BOARD command to set up a position.

SELF = 1¶
WALL = 3¶

Convert to Gomocup BOARD format.

String in format “x,y,colorn…” with DONE terminator.

class pygomo.protocol.Evaluate(raw_value: str, DEFAULT_SCALING_FACTOR: float = 400.0)[source]¶

Bases: object

Engine evaluation score with winrate conversion.

Supports both centipawn scores and mate scores.

DEFAULT_SCALING_FACTOR: float = 400.0¶
is_losing() → bool[source]¶

Check if position is losing (negative mate).

is_mate() → bool[source]¶

Check if this is a mate score.

is_winning() → bool[source]¶

Check if position is winning (positive mate).

raw_value: str¶
score() → int | float | MateScore[source]¶

Get the evaluation score.

Returns:

Numeric score, or MateScore for mate positions.

winrate(scaling_factor: float | None = None) → float[source]¶

Convert evaluation to winrate using sigmoid function.

Formula: winrate = 1 / (1 + e^(-eval / scaling_factor))

Parameters:

scaling_factor – Controls curve steepness. Higher = flatter.

Returns:

Winrate in range [0, 1].

winrate_percent(scaling_factor: float | None = None) → float[source]¶

Get winrate as percentage [0, 100].

class pygomo.protocol.GomocupProtocol[source]¶

Bases: IProtocol

Gomocup (Piskvork) protocol implementation.

Handles serialization and parsing of Gomocup protocol messages. Supports both standard Gomocup and Yixin-Board extensions.

Example

protocol = GomocupProtocol() cmd = protocol.serialize_command(“TURN”, “7,8”) # Output: “TURN 7,8”

RESPONSE_TYPES = {'ABOUT': ResponseType.TEXT, 'BEGIN': ResponseType.COORD, 'BOARD': ResponseType.COORD, 'END': ResponseType.NONE, 'INFO': ResponseType.NONE, 'RESTART': ResponseType.OK, 'START': ResponseType.OK, 'STOP': ResponseType.NONE, 'TAKEBACK': ResponseType.OK, 'TURN': ResponseType.COORD, 'YXBOARD': ResponseType.NONE, 'YXNBEST': ResponseType.COORD, 'YXSHOWFORBID': ResponseType.TEXT, 'YXSHOWINFO': ResponseType.MESSAGE, 'YXSTOP': ResponseType.NONE}¶
THINKING_COMMANDS = frozenset({'BEGIN', 'BOARD', 'SWAP2BOARD', 'TURN', 'YXBALANCEONE', 'YXBALANCETWO', 'YXNBEST', 'YXSEARCHDEFEND'})¶

Parse coordinate string to (x, y).

data – Coordinate string like “7,8”.

parse_message(data: str) → dict[str, Any][source]¶

Parse a MESSAGE line into structured data.

Handles multiple formats from Rapfi: - UCILIKE: depth X-Y ev VAL n NODES n/ms SPEED tm TIME pv MOVES - UCILIKE MCTS: multipv X ev VAL w WR d DR stdev SD v V seldepth SD n N n/ms SPEED tm TIME prior P pv MOVES - NORMAL: Depth X-Y | Eval VAL | Time T | MOVES

Parameters:

data – Raw MESSAGE line.

Returns:

Dictionary with parsed fields.

parse_search_info(data: str) → SearchInfo[source]¶

Parse MESSAGE line into SearchInfo object.

Parameters:

data – Raw MESSAGE line.

SearchInfo with parsed data.

Serialize a command with arguments.

Parameters:

  • command – Command name.

  • *args – Command arguments.

Returns:

Formatted command string.

class pygomo.protocol.IProtocol[source]¶

Bases: ABC

Interface for engine communication protocol.

Handles command serialization and response parsing. Implementations should be stateless and thread-safe.

True if the command makes the engine think.

data – Coordinate string (e.g., “7,8”).

Tuple of (x, y) coordinates.

abstractmethod parse_message(data: str) → dict[str, Any][source]¶

Parse a MESSAGE line into structured data.

Parameters:

data – The MESSAGE line from engine.

Dictionary with parsed fields.

The formatted command string ready for transmission.

class pygomo.protocol.Move(move: tuple[int, int] | str)[source]¶

Bases: object

Represents a board position/move.

Supports multiple input formats:

Coordinates are 0-indexed internally.

INFO = 5¶
MESSAGE = 4¶
MULTI_COORD = 3¶
NONE = 8¶
OK = 1¶
TEXT = 7¶
class pygomo.protocol.SearchInfo(depth: int = 0, sel_depth: int = 0, eval: Evaluate = <factory>, nodes: int = 0, nps: int = 0, time_ms: int = 0, pv: list[Move] = <factory>, multipv: int = 1)[source]¶

Bases: object

Parsed search information from engine MESSAGE output.

Contains realtime search progress data including depth, evaluation, nodes, speed, and principal variation.

depth: int = 0¶
eval: Evaluate¶
multipv: int = 1¶
nodes: int = 0¶
nps: int = 0¶
pv: list[Move]¶
sel_depth: int = 0¶
time_ms: int = 0¶
property winrate: float¶

Get winrate from evaluation.

property winrate_percent: float¶

Get winrate as percentage.

Transport¶

Transport layer provides abstractions for engine process communication.

class pygomo.transport.IStreamReader[source]¶

Bases: ABC

Interface for reading from engine output stream.

Implementations should handle buffering and line-based reading.

abstractmethod close() → None[source]¶

Close the reader and release resources.

abstractmethod readline(timeout: float | None = None) → str[source]¶

Read a single line from the stream.

Parameters:

timeout – Maximum time to wait in seconds. None means no timeout.

Returns:

The line read (without trailing newline), or empty string on EOF.

Raises:

TransportTimeoutError – If timeout expires before data is available.

class pygomo.transport.IStreamWriter[source]¶

Bases: ABC

Interface for writing to engine input stream.

abstractmethod close() → None[source]¶

Close the writer and release resources.

abstractmethod flush() → None[source]¶

Flush the write buffer.

abstractmethod writeline(data: str) → None[source]¶

Write a line to the stream.

Parameters:

data – The data to write (newline will be appended automatically).

Raises:

TransportError – If write fails.

class pygomo.transport.ITransport[source]¶

Bases: ABC

Interface for engine transport layer.

Manages the lifecycle of engine process and provides read/write access to stdin/stdout streams.

abstractmethod get_reader() → IStreamReader[source]¶

Get the stream reader for advanced usage.

abstractmethod get_writer() → IStreamWriter[source]¶

Get the stream writer for advanced usage.

abstract property is_running: bool¶

Check if the engine process is currently running.

abstract property process_id: int | None¶

Get the process ID of the engine, or None if not running.

abstractmethod receive(timeout: float | None = None) → str[source]¶

Receive a line from the engine’s stdout.

Parameters:

timeout – Maximum time to wait in seconds.

Returns:

The received line, or empty string on EOF.

Raises:

  • TransportTimeoutError – If timeout expires.

  • TransportError – If engine is not running.

abstractmethod send(data: str) → None[source]¶

Send data to the engine’s stdin.

Parameters:

data – The command to send (newline will be appended).

Raises:

TransportError – If engine is not running.

abstractmethod start() → None[source]¶

Start the engine process.

Raises:

  • TransportConnectionError – If the engine fails to start.

  • TransportError – If the engine is already running.

abstractmethod stop(timeout: float = 5.0) → None[source]¶

Stop the engine process gracefully.

Parameters:

timeout – Maximum time to wait for graceful shutdown.

class pygomo.transport.OutputChannelRouter(stream: TextIO)[source]¶

Bases: object

Routes engine stdout lines to categorized channels.

Each channel has a filter function that determines which lines belong to it. Lines are read in a background thread and distributed to the appropriate queues.

Default channels:

  • coord: Lines matching coordinate pattern (e.g., “7,8”)

  • message: Lines starting with “MESSAGE”

  • info: Lines starting with “INFO”

  • error: Lines starting with “ERROR”

  • output: All other non-error lines

Example

router = OutputChannelRouter(process.stdout)

# Get a coordinate response coord = router.get(“coord”, timeout=5.0)

# Get realtime search info message = router.get(“message”, timeout=0.1)

COORD_PATTERN = re.compile('^\\d+\\s*,\\s*\\d+(\\s+\\d+\\s*,\\s*\\d+)*$')¶
add_channel(name: str, filter_func: Callable[[str], bool]) → None[source]¶

Add a new output channel.

Parameters:

  • name – Channel name for retrieval.

  • filter_func – Function that returns True if a line belongs to this channel.

Raises:

ValueError – If channel already exists.

property channels: list[str]¶

Get list of available channel names.

clear(channel: str) → None[source]¶

Clear all pending messages in a channel.

Parameters:

channel – Channel name to clear.

clear_all() → None[source]¶

Clear all pending messages in all channels.

get(channel: str, timeout: float = 0.0, reset: bool = False) → str[source]¶

Get the next line from a channel.

Parameters:

  • channel – Channel name to read from.

  • timeout – Maximum time to wait in seconds. 0 means no wait.

  • reset – If True, clear old messages before reading.

Returns:

The next line, or empty string on timeout.

Raises:

ValueError – If channel doesn’t exist.

get_all(channel: str) → list[str][source]¶

Get all available lines from a channel.

Parameters:

channel – Channel name to read from.

Returns:

List of all available lines.

get_nowait(channel: str) → str | None[source]¶

Get a line without waiting.

Parameters:

channel – Channel name to read from.

Returns:

The next line, or None if queue is empty.

remove_channel(name: str) → None[source]¶

Remove an output channel.

Parameters:

name – Channel name to remove.

stop() → None[source]¶

Stop the background reader thread.

class pygomo.transport.SubprocessTransport(executable_path: str, args: list[str] | None = None, working_directory: str | None = None)[source]¶

Bases: ITransport

Transport implementation using Python subprocess.

Spawns an engine executable and provides bidirectional communication via stdin/stdout pipes.

Example

transport = SubprocessTransport(“/path/to/engine”) transport.start() transport.send(“START 15”) response = transport.receive(timeout=5.0) transport.stop()

get_reader() → IStreamReader[source]¶

Get a reader for the default channel.

get_router() → OutputChannelRouter[source]¶

Get the output channel router for advanced usage.

Returns:

The OutputChannelRouter instance.

get_writer() → IStreamWriter[source]¶

Get the stdin writer.

property is_running: bool¶

Check if the engine process is running.

property process_id: int | None¶

Get the process ID of the engine.

receive(timeout: float | None = None) → str[source]¶

Receive a line from the engine’s default output.

Parameters:

timeout – Timeout in seconds.

Returns:

The received line.

send(data: str) → None[source]¶

Send a command to the engine.

Parameters:

data – Command string (newline appended automatically).

start() → None[source]¶

Start the engine subprocess.

Raises:

  • TransportError – If already running.

  • TransportConnectionError – If process fails to start.

stop(timeout: float = 5.0) → None[source]¶

Stop the engine subprocess.

Attempts graceful shutdown first, then forces termination.

Parameters:

timeout – Time to wait for graceful shutdown.

exception pygomo.transport.TransportError[source]¶

Bases: Exception

Base exception for transport layer errors.

exception pygomo.transport.TransportTimeoutError[source]¶

Bases: TransportError

Raised when a transport operation times out.
